checks.finding: REQ-001 (fail-closed exit code + zero-mutation via git status --porcelain, AC-001..006), REQ-002 (needs: dependency + text-marker checks, AC-007..010), REQ-003 (CHANGELOG entry + doc existence, AC-011..012), REQ-004 (non-existence assertion + doc marker, AC-013..014) are each observable/measurable with concrete downstream test hooks.
REQ-001->AC-001..006, REQ-002->AC-007..010, REQ-003->AC-011..012, REQ-004->AC-013..014; acceptance-tests.md table confirms 1:1 AC-to-TEST mapping with no orphaned goal or AC.

All 14 ACs specify externally observable outcomes (exit codes, git status --porcelain byte-identity, grep/text-marker matches on real source files, file existence/content markers) rather than intent statements.

Non-goals section explicitly excludes the wider risk-adaptive-layer release-gating scope (INV-015), budget harmonization (OQ-005), timing emission (OQ-006), bump-version.ps1 authoring, workflow_run trigger broadening, protected-gate-file edits, and Phase 2 artifacts.

Bash-only/no-.ps1 degradation is an explicit recorded design decision (REQ-004/OQ-003); fail-closed-no-bypass is explicit (OQ-007/AC-004); Security Boundaries table (B1-B4) states trust boundaries and data classification; approval boundary (human maintainer approves spec/tasks, publishes releases) is explicit in Roles and Permissions.

High risk (silent bypass) maps to AC-004/AC-008/AC-009; Medium risk (shared CHANGELOG/registration collision) maps to the now-consistent create-then-append + T-002-Blocker-on-T-001 serialization plus AC-011/012 end-state assertion; Medium risk (fixture path resolution) maps to AC-001's happy-path canary; Low risk (scope creep) maps to Non-goals. The round-1 CHANGELOG ownership contradiction (Main Workflows vs Edge Cases) is resolved and no longer undermines this validation surface.
